methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Ultra Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $3M to date, led by founder Max Friefeld. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector comprises 1 public comparable (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics, though no narrow peer set of same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with sufficient data density exists for direct median comparison.

Given data constraints, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $3M raised baseline, implying an $8M valuation. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable; the broader sector contains 11 robotics peers but none satisfy minimum thresholds (three or more datapoints) when filtered for stage alignment or valuation tier similarity. Using all-sector medians would introduce material bias given stage and business-model variance within robotics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M (low) to $9M (high), with a median point of $8M. This range reflects a 2.0x to 2.9x multiple on capital raised, consistent with early-stage risk and market positioning, but remains reliant on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable Seed-stage robotics valuations to validate the 2.5x multiplier and limited visibility into Ultra Robotics' revenue, unit economics, or technology differentiation relative to named peers.
